qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated HTTP stager brute force discloses staged implant payloads and embedded secrets
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory fixes unbounded gzip decompression in `util/encoders/gzip.go` and bounded request-body decoding in `server/c2/http.go`; this finding instead relies on unauthenticated numeric resource-ID brute force and missing authorization on stage retrieval.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable gzip decode root cause in `util/encoders/gzip.go` or the corresponding HTTP request-body decode path that permitted zip-bomb denial of service.
evidence: The GHSA and upstream patch `0cf5a47cfdf94b6ab481ec3ea0db09f31654c0f0` show the advisory root cause is unbounded gzip decoding in `util/encoders/gzip.go`, plus `server/c2/http.go` callers that previously used `encoder.Decode(body)` without a decoded-size limit.

The only final qualifying finding in the run manifest, `http_stager_bruteforce_stage_disclosure`, is a confidentiality issue caused by unauthenticated stage lookup and payload serving via numeric resource-ID enumeration, not by gzip decoding or resource exhaustion.
